_meta.source: Public podcast pages + audit conducted during artifact build
_meta.warning: Audience numbers are public estimates and shift quickly. Re-check the show's media kit before a final pitch. Hosts and contact channels are public information; recent_episode_url points to a concrete recent episode at the time of curation.

no_priors.name: No Priors
no_priors.focus: Frontier AI, agent infrastructure, foundation-model startups
no_priors.audience_size: Top-10 tech podcast on Spotify; recurring 50k+ download episodes
no_priors.host_angle: Sarah pushes deep technical implementation; Elad pushes economics + go-to-market. Both interested in agent orchestration architecture, not generic LLM chat.
no_priors.recent_episode_url: https://www.sierra.ai/resources/podcasts (Bret Taylor episode used as Typewise's positioning anchor)
no_priors.contact_hint: Sarah Guo on X @saranormous; Elad Gil at elad.eladgil.com — pitch via X DM preferred
no_priors.recommended_typewise_angle: Multi-agent orchestration that ships INSIDE existing helpdesks instead of replacing them — the 'augment vs replace' thesis Sierra+Decagon don't credibly play. ETH Zurich co-development gives a deep-tech anchor.

the_cx_cast.name: The CX Cast
the_cx_cast.focus: Customer experience strategy for Fortune 500 buyers
the_cx_cast.audience_size: Forrester analyst audience; B2B enterprise CS leaders
the_cx_cast.host_angle: Forrester is data-led: bring research, comparative metrics, named customer outcomes. They distrust pitches without numbers.
the_cx_cast.contact_hint: Pitch via Forrester analyst inquiry → CX practice; or direct LinkedIn to a current host (lineup rotates)
the_cx_cast.recommended_typewise_angle: Brack.ch case study: multilingual DACH retail, AI agent on top of Zendesk, deflection metric. EU data residency as enterprise differentiator vs US-hosted Fin/Decagon.

modern_customer_podcast.name: The Modern Customer Podcast
modern_customer_podcast.focus: Customer experience trends, AI in CX, future-of-work
modern_customer_podcast.audience_size: 5+ years running, Forbes column synergy, 100k+ monthly LinkedIn reach for the host
modern_customer_podcast.host_angle: Blake favours examples over abstractions. She wants a customer story with a number attached.
modern_customer_podcast.contact_hint: blakemorgan.com pitch form; very responsive on LinkedIn DMs
modern_customer_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: How a 200-integration AI agent layer ships inside Zendesk in days, not the 6-month migration timeline of replacement platforms. Lead with Brack.ch or Galaxus.

be_customer_led.name: Be Customer Led
be_customer_led.focus: Operating CX at scale; CX-as-a-discipline inside B2B
be_customer_led.audience_size: Niche but high signal — CS/CX directors and VPs as listener base
be_customer_led.host_angle: Bill cares about CX ops and tooling decisions. The 'augment, don't replace' framing fits his audience perfectly.
be_customer_led.contact_hint: Bill Staikos on LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/billstaikos), highly responsive to direct pitches
be_customer_led.recommended_typewise_angle: Why CS directors should add AI as a helpdesk layer (not a rip-and-replace), with EU data residency as the board-defensible compliance angle.

punk_cx.name: Punk CX
punk_cx.focus: Contrarian CX takes, AI augmentation, anti-incumbent angles
punk_cx.host_angle: Adrian likes provocations. Lead with what's wrong with the current AI-in-CS narrative, then position Typewise as the fix.
punk_cx.recommended_typewise_angle: Contrarian: most AI CS vendors are selling chatbot replacement; Typewise is the layer that respects the helpdesk you already paid for. EU-hosted by design, not as an afterthought.

support_driven_podcast.name: Support Driven Podcast
support_driven_podcast.host: Jay Nathan, Andrea Saez (and community guests)
support_driven_podcast.focus: Customer support leadership, team operations, tool decisions
support_driven_podcast.audience_size: Connected to the Support Driven Slack (10,000+ CS pros) — direct buyer reach
support_driven_podcast.host_angle: Practitioners. They want to hear what tooling actually moved an agent's day, not what the marketing deck says.
support_driven_podcast.contact_hint: Direct LinkedIn to Jay Nathan; or via Support Driven Slack #podcast channel
support_driven_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: Frontline-agent perspective: how an agent assist layer on Zendesk changes the volume per agent per day. Bring an agent (not a marketer) on the episode.

twenty_vc.name: 20VC
twenty_vc.audience_size: One of the highest-reach VC podcasts; Fortune 500 / VC LP listener base
twenty_vc.host_angle: Harry likes founder-origin stories with a clean inflection. The B2C-keyboard-to-B2B-agent pivot is exactly the narrative arc that works here.
twenty_vc.contact_hint: Harry Stebbings on X @HarryStebbings, very accessible by DM if pitch is sharp
twenty_vc.recommended_typewise_angle: Pivot story: B2C Typewise keyboard (2M downloads) → B2B agent platform → 50+ enterprise customers including Unilever / DPD. ETH Zurich research roots. EU deep-tech.

saastr_podcast.name: SaaStr Podcast
saastr_podcast.focus: B2B SaaS scaling, founder-led GTM, ARR milestones
saastr_podcast.audience_size: SaaStr community is the canonical B2B SaaS audience; ~100k podcast subs
saastr_podcast.host_angle: Jason is a numbers guy. Come with ARR trajectory, customer count, NRR, or don't come. Pivot stories also welcomed.
saastr_podcast.contact_hint: saastr.com/podcast-pitch form; or LinkedIn DM to Jason Lemkin
saastr_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: EU-anchored B2B SaaS that wins enterprise without migrating off Salesforce or Zendesk. Public customer logos: Unilever, DPD, TUI, IVECO, Beurer, Galaxus, Brack.ch.

lennys_podcast.name: Lenny's Podcast
lennys_podcast.focus: Product, growth, B2B SaaS — operator interviews
lennys_podcast.host_angle: Lenny wants operator playbooks, not vision. The Typewise growth angle should be 'how a 20-person company is competing with Sierra/Decagon on a non-paid channel strategy.'
lennys_podcast.contact_hint: Lenny Rachitsky on LinkedIn or via lennysnewsletter.com pitch form
lennys_podcast.recommended_typewise_angle: Growth playbook: how non-paid channels (MCP server for devs, founder LinkedIn, DACH community) win against well-funded US competitors. Show the radar.

acquired.name: Acquired
acquired.focus: Long-form company histories, strategy, tech ecosystems
acquired.audience_size: Top-5 business podcast globally; Fortune 500 + VC + tech-founder reach
acquired.host_angle: They cover scaled companies, not pre-Series-B startups. Pitch only when Typewise has a more public narrative arc (e.g. Series B announced). Useful to watch as a future channel, not today.
acquired.contact_hint: ben@acquired.fm — but unlikely to land at Typewise's current stage; track for later
acquired.recommended_typewise_angle: Reserved for after a major funding or growth milestone. Today: track and plant the narrative seed (B2C-to-B2B pivot, ETH research, EU enterprise differentiator).
